source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Pipeline Leak

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Grand Isle Block 76

actions_taken: Pressure Safety Low (PSL) trip activated, shutting in platform
Operators bled down pipeline to stop additional pollution
Field Foreman conducted fly-over to estimate sheen size
Pollution event reported to National Response Center (NRC #1222650) and BSEE
Dive boat contracted to identify leak location
Diver identified leak near weld approximately quarter-mile from platform
Fieldwood monitored sheen hourly from discovery until dissipation
Pipeline repair plan submitted to BSEE for split-sleeve clamp installation
12-inch Plidco split-sleeve clamp installed and leak tested at 1060 psi
Pipeline buried 3 feet below mudline after successful test
event.summary: On 25 August 2018 at 0730 hours, a 3/8-inch hole developed in a 12-inch departing pipeline (KAH-101, segment #8217) at Fieldwood Energy's Grand Isle 76-A platform, discharging approximately 8.35 barrels (352.27 gallons) of hydrocarbons into the Gulf of Mexico. The Pressure Safety Low sensor detected the leak and shut in the platform. Investigation revealed the pipeline had been converted from gas sales/condensate service to bulk oil service without BSEE approval. Contributing factors included improper Pressure Safety High (PSH) set point at 1200 psi (too close to MAOP of 1203 psi) and improper Pressure Safety Valve (PSV) set point at 1323 psi, both failing to provide adequate overpressure protection. The pipeline pumps' Maximum Discharge Pressure of 1800 psi exceeded the pipeline's MAOP, creating overpressure risk.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District Office referred findings to BSEE regional pipeline division for further review of Fieldwood Energy's pipeline modifications
BSEE Accident Investigator referred discrepancies on Safety Function Diagram (SFD) and Safety Analysis Function Evaluation (SAFE) chart to Production Operations Section Chief for operator correction

threats.name: Overpressure event due to improper PSH set point
Inadequate secondary overpressure protection (PSV set too high)
Pipeline material failure near weld due to stress concentration
Unauthorized pipeline service conversion without engineering review

consequences.name: Loss of containment - hydrocarbon discharge to environment
Marine pollution - 8.35 barrels discharged to Gulf of Mexico
Environmental impact - sheen covering 9.75 square miles

controls.name: Pressure Safety Low (PSL) sensor and automatic shutdown

supporting_text: The pipeline pressure sensor detected the leak, shutting in the platform
a Pressure Safety Low (PSL) trip on the departing 12" pipeline, KAH-101 segment #8217

controls.name: Pressure Safety High (PSH) - primary overpressure protection
controls.lod_basis: API RP 14C requirement for primary overpressure protection

supporting_text: The pipeline's primary means of overpressure protection was the PSH set at 1200 psi. Although this set point is below the MAOP of the pipeline, it is too close to MAOP to allow for set pressure tolerance
the pipeline pumps may have overpressured the pipeline without activating the PSH due to the MAOP of the pipeline being within 5 percent of the set point
The improper PSH set point has been identified as a possible contributing cause to the incident because an overpressure event may have gone undetected and caused a hole in the pipeline

controls.name: Pressure Safety Valve (PSV) - secondary overpressure protection
controls.lod_basis: API RP 14C requirement for secondary overpressure protection

supporting_text: the pumps' PSV was set at 1323 psi which does not sufficiently protect the pipeline's MAOP at 1203 psi
Therefore, the pipelines did not have proper secondary overpressure protection
The improper PSV set point on the pipeline pumps has also been identified as a possible contributing cause

controls.name: Pipeline modification approval and engineering review process
controls.barrier_role: Ensure pipeline design and protection systems match service conditions
controls.lod_basis: 30 CFR 250.1000(3) requirement for BSEE approval of pipeline modifications

supporting_text: Fieldwood Energy converted the pipeline to a bulk oil pipeline without BSEE's approval, failing to comply with 30 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) 250.1000 (3) Modification or relinquishment of a pipeline right-of way
This pipeline was originally used for gas sales and condensate
Pipeline segment #8217 was being used as a bulk oil pipeline; however, the pipeline was originally permitted to handle gas and condensate only

controls.name: Oil spill response and reporting procedures
controls.barrier_role: Detect, report, and respond to pollution events
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement for spill reporting and response

supporting_text: The interviews revealed the operators followed Fieldwood Energy's oil spill procedure
The Field Foreman reported the pollution event to the National Response Center (NRC #1222650) as well as the BSEE New Orleans District after hours engineer
Fieldwood began monitoring the sheen hourly from the time of its discovery at 10:50 am on 25 August 2018 until the sheen had completely dissipated at 3:00 pm on 26 August 2018

controls.name: Dive inspection and leak location identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify and locate source of leak for repair

supporting_text: Fieldwood called for a dive boat contracted to Fieldwood. Upon arrival, the diver identified the leak near a weld located approximately a quarter-mile from the platform
A 3/8" hole developed in the departing pipeline which discharged an estimated 8.35 barrels of hydrocarbons into the Gulf of Mexico (GOM)

controls.name: Pipeline repair and pressure testing
controls.barrier_role: Restore pipeline integrity and prevent recurrence

supporting_text: Fieldwood submitted a pipeline repair plan to the BSEE Pipeline Section to install a 12" Plidco split-sleeve clamp. BSEE gave approval for the repair on 28 August 2018

The clamp was installed and leak tested at the normal operating pressure of 1060 psi for 2 hours while being observed by the diver. After a successful test, the pipeline was bled down to ambient pressure and buried 3' below the mudline

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
